title: Cartesia launches Sonic-3.5 and Ink-2 for real-time voice AI
friendlyTitle: Cartesia launches faster speech models for voice agents
summary: Cartesia launched Sonic-3.5 for text-to-speech and Ink-2 for speech-to-text, positioning them as a combined low-latency stack for voice agents. The company claims sub-90ms TTS latency, 100ms transcript latency, native turn detection, and support for more than 40 languages in Sonic-3.5.
friendlySummary: Cartesia released new speech-to-text and text-to-speech models for real-time voice AI. The company is pitching them as a full stack for natural, low-latency voice agents.
whyItMatters: Voice agents depend on the full conversational loop: hearing, deciding, and speaking quickly enough to feel natural. Better STT and TTS stacks can improve phone support, accessibility tools, tutoring, and hands-free workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Voice AI gets useful when it stops feeling delayed. These models could help businesses build better phone agents, tutors, and accessibility tools.
